review: Stephanie’s place was very clean and spacious. It was conveniently located and easy to navigate the city from this location. It had all the necessary amenities needed for our stay. Stephanie was a great host!  Would recommend staying here.

description: Be our guest in Albany! Thoughtfully furnished 1BR/1BA APT . Centrally situated In Melrose neighborhood, one of the best neighborhoods Albany has to offer. Close proximity to major Hospitals, Medical centers and University. Take a short walk to restaurants or the beautiful Buckingham Pond walking paths. Unit sleeps two or have some guests visit and utilize the pull out couch in the Livingroom. Full Kitchen with breakfast bar that could double as a great location for remote working.
review: I stayed in this unit for almost three months (two bookings back to back). Riccardo is a great host. He took care of a small issue immediately. The neighborhood is safe and the off street parking was appreciated. The unit is well cared for and clean. It has a cozy feel. I appreciated the snacks and other thoughtful items provided to make my stay comfortable. I would recommend this unit and Riccardo to other travelers to the Albany area.

description: Keep it simple at this peaceful and centrally-located place. Very spacious apartment close to Albany Medical center, colleges, shopping and eateries. Come stay and relax after a long day of work or school. We have plenty off street parking available.
review: Had some issues at check in which were eventually resolved. Listed unit was different then one given. It was just a different unit in the same building but I think that’s what caused the check in issue.  Mine was 3rd floor fyi. Arbor hill isn’t the nicest location, I had noisy neighbors and a decent amount of city noise in general. Walls are thin. Building is old like most others in the area,  floors aren’t at all level and the second room door sticks terribly. I had to use my entire body weight to shut it or open it. Unit currently doesn’t have any window ac units and NY gets hot, host did say they’d have provided one if they knew I was uncomfortable during my stay. Bed currently is unstable, again host said they’d address that if they knew, but that requires a new bed. My stay was long term which I do not suggest, for a day or two this would be ok. The noise level is hit or miss, but if you’re here for a week+ you’ll notice for sure. Not recommended if you’re a light sleeper.

description: Quiet suburban neighborhood minutes from downtown Albany. Guests have access to the kitchen, dinette and deck. Garage space is also available.  Private guest quarters include a large bedroom with a queen-size bed, desk and private bath. Guests also enjoy their own spacious living area with a large TV, massage chair, convertible couch, microwave, water cooler and exercise equipment.
review: Perfect base camp for a very stressful couple of days at the state capitol. Rachel is an incredibly considerate host and the room was great. I would absolutely recommend staying here to anyone spending time in Albany!

description: Modern townhouse across from the Governors mansion. This unique property offers a perfect blend of historic charm and contemporary living in a building that whispers stories of the past.  A city retreat offering a blend of security, and accessibility to the city's key attractions.  1,900 sq ft of living space includes a modern living/dining area. A spacious 2nd floor bedroom and separate office and library. Large windows fill the spaces with light and access to a large outdoor yard.
review: Loved my brief stay in Robert’s beautiful apartment. On a lovely street with historic sights viewable right from the window.  Extremely spacious, comfortable, and very clean.

description: Enjoy this lovely neighborhood of Albany when you stay in this 2-bedroom, 2 bathroom home! Bring the whole family to this great place with lots of room for fun. This bright and cozy home includes a fully equipped kitchen, washer/dryer in the home, a huge backyard with a privacy fence and a swing set. A newly remodeled second floor provides a suite-like experience for anyone staying there.   A great place for a family, traveling professionals, or a single person looking for lots of space.
review: This stay was absolutely incredible. The house is gorgeous with perfect personal touches (video game console,dvd movies, games, etc), as well as super spacious and perfect for 2-5 guests. I couldn’t recommended this place enough, thinking about booking a reservation right now due to my great experience! I will say this is a pet-allowed home so if your at all allergic to dog/cat hair as I am maybe look for a different option but not booking this stay would leave you with so much regret

description: This fully furnished 2-bedroom bungalow is ideal for monthly stays (30+ nights) and includes utilities, high-speed Wi-Fi, and in-unit laundry. Located just 10 minutes from Albany Medical Center and St. Peter’s Health Partners, it's perfect for travel nurses, digital nomads, corporate professionals, relocating families, or those visiting for medical care. A comfortable and convenient home base for work, family, or exploring New York’s capital region.
review: My family of 3 had a good stay here.  You can tell the owner has put a lot of effort into updating the interior with nice furnishings.  While there is no dedicated parking space for the home, we were able to find parking right across the street each day except for trash day (Sunday evening-Monday morning).  If you plan to stay over that night, you will walk a little further.  We did have to park just over a block away.  The neighborhood is older, which may be a turnoff for some but we felt safe while there and found the street to have minimal traffic noise which allowed us sleep well.  Dashima made sure we knew how to contact her if we had a need and that was appreciated.  I would stay here again when in the area.

Our room was one of the smaller ones but was tastefully decorated and the linens and bathroom were immaculate. Location is literally right next to the park so we enjoyed a nice morning walk. Highly recommend!

review: I needed a place to tie me over until I could move into my apartment and Emily's spot fit that perfectly. I was new to the area and Emily was a great host by providing local recommendations and showing hospitality. I enjoyed my stay and would recommend it to anyone else.

review: Lizas home was just as advertised. Close enough to everything in town yet in a nice and private area. We are grateful for Liza’s responsiveness to my questions and she was only helpful. My toddler loved the yard and enjoyed playing the piano for the first time. The bidet gave my very country living family a good laugh. Very clear instructions and the house had everything we needed and more! Thanks again Liza!

review: Musab is an excellent host and his place is great for the price you pay and the location you’re in. Public transport is easily accessible, and the area is pretty safe and walkable with a beautiful park right outside.  The rooms overall are nice and clean, the mattress and pillows were comfortable, and there was ample closet space. The walls definitely would benefit from a paint job though.  The kitchen is very well equipped and the shared bathroom is clean, you may have to wait a couple of minutes in the morning if someone else gets in before you.  Overall the safety and privacy is excellent, and the host is extremely friendly and cooperative (he helped me out with a bunch of things on the same day i requested him), and I will definitely be coming back again!

Did have some issues with parking like having to drive for 5 minutes or more to find somewhere to park after work or park a street over or down. But other than that had no complaints.

Your booking includes free parking and a complimentary breakfast to start your day right. Located near shops, restaurants, and downtown Albany, this home offers easy access to local attractions. Whether you’re here for business or leisure, you’ll love the comfort and convenience of this stay. Book now for a relaxing experience.
review: The room did what we needed it to do. It’s located inside a run-down hotel with a 2.2-star rating on Google, which made us a bit nervous going in. The photos made the room look amazing, but in reality, it was much more worn down and clearly in need of renovations. That said, the room was much cleaner than expected given the hotel’s reputation. There was a lingering stench throughout the entire building, including the room, but it was tolerable. The beds were comfortable, the AC worked great, and the Wi-Fi was solid. The TV, however, looked like it hadn’t been updated in 15 years. The location was decent, and the room ultimately served its purpose for our stay. Just know that what you see in the pictures isn’t exactly what you’ll get. Still, the room itself was in better condition than the hotel’s reviews make it sound.

description: Enjoy a stylish experience at this centrally-located place. The unit is newly renovated with a 4K Smart TV, in unit washer/dryer, updated kitchen and bathroom, high speed internet.   You will also have access to the building gym.
review: The space is VERY spacious and nice.  Everything was very clean.  We would stay there again.

This unit also has a private laundry room as well as an entrance foyer. There is off street parking and the building is located within walking distance to 2 primary bus routes. This unit is also within walking distance of popular restaurants, Juniors, The Point, Cafe Madison and Kismet Mediterranean Grill.
review: We stayed for 22 days and would definitely return. David was extremely helpful and always responded quickly to our questions. The house is in a beautiful and quiet neighborhood in Albany, and every morning we woke up to birds singing. We were there while my daughter was doing an exchange program at UAlbany, and the house was perfect for our needs. The living room is cozy, with a comfortable sofa, reclining chair, TV, and desk. The kitchen is modern and fully equipped with everything you need for a long stay. It includes great appliances, a stove, a good fridge, dishwasher, washer and dryer. The three bedrooms have double beds. Just one note: the mattresses are very soft, which might not suit people with back issues. Bathrooms are clean and the showers have great water pressure. Thank you, David, we had unforgettable moments in your home!

description: This 3-bedroom, 1-bath Empire State apartment serves up not only all the comforts of home, but also an ideal location. Energize your days with a cup of coffee in the comfort of your own vacation rental, before heading out to explore the New York State Museum, Irish American Heritage Museum, and even the Albany Institute of History & Art. Later, head to Pizzeria Sapienza for a slice of true New York pie, or slurp down some oysters at Jack’s Oyster House!
review: Good news first… the host was easy to communicate with and checked on us each day to see if needed anything. She also provided snacks, fruit and a refrigerator full of bottled water, which we totally appreciated. Having a washer / dryer with laundry detergent in the unit was a plus. The rental was very clean. Linens were nice and plentiful. The labels where things were located were helpful.  Now for the not so good news… We paid $577 after cleaning and service fee for two nights. Really felt this was very overpriced for location and condition of unit. (It was extremely hot. Lighting was terribly dark. Mattresses and pillows were not that comfortable. Didn't care for the sketchy, downtown Albany location.) We could have stayed in two adjoining, 4-star hotel rooms for less than $500 total and enjoyed air conditioning, an indoor pool and free breakfast! Definitely regretting that we did not investigate that option before booking this Airbnb.  This rental unit is on the top floor (3rd level) of an old, downtown Albany brownstone. The stairs are narrow and could be difficult for some to navigate. The flooring of the unit is uneven in many spots and there is a small 2" step up from the kitchen to the main area that we all tripped on at least 3 times each. Once I almost spilled a bowl of hot soup walking from the kitchen to the dining room because I forgot it was there.  It took almost 6 hours for one small, undersized A/C unit to cool down the common space and as such, it made for very uncomfortable sleeping the first night. It was 87 degrees when we arrived at 7:30 pm and it finally cooled to 73 degrees by the morning. There really needs to be an additional A/C unit for the kitchen area to help with the heat issue. We had to leave all bedroom doors open with the fans blowing into each room to help draw the cool air in. I slept with a cold washcloth on my forehead and a cold water bottle from the refrigerator on my neck the first night in an effort to cool down.  The hostess told us there was an additional bed rolled up in one of the closets. We were expecting an air mattress; however, it turned out to be a metal folding bed frame (4” thick), which was useless. Even with the 3” foam mattress we found and another thinner latex mattress topper, and 2 thick comforters piled together, my 11-year-old son could still feel the metal frame. We ended up putting the frame back in the closet and he just slept on the floor on the foam and comforters.  The interior lighting is very dark making the space feel dingy. All the incandescent light bulbs (which contribute to the heat problem) need to be replaced with daylight LEDs. Some of the furniture is uncomfortable, like the one chair whose springs are all broken and poke you in the butt when you sit on it or the desk chair that squeaks loudly or the wobbly dining room chair that has been mended. Actually, the entire dining room set reminded me of something you’d find in the employee lunchroom at a Walmart. The furniture felt like it was obtained at a garage sale or second hand store… kind of cheap and past its prime.      The description of the apartment being 3,000 square feet is wrong. The unit might be 1,000 square feet, but certainly not 3,000... Perhaps that is the size of the entire 3 story building? Also, there was mention in the Airbnb description of a pool and gym in / near the building. When I asked the host about this, she said there are neither of these close by.   We’ve had many, many great Airbnb experiences, which is why I always search for Airbnb lodging before a hotel… but this Airbnb experience was not worth the $288 we paid per night and we would have better served at a hotel.

description: Enjoy a comfortable stay in this cozy Albany hotel room with free breakfast and free parking included. Conveniently located, it’s perfect for travelers looking for a relaxing and affordable stay. The room is clean, well-maintained, and offers essential amenities for a pleasant experience. Whether you’re visiting for business or leisure, you’ll appreciate the easy access to local attractions, dining, and shopping. Book now for a hassle-free stay with great perks!
review: I usually don’t like giving bad review, but I also like to be very honest I would never be renting this hotel is very old nothing it’s updated there. The room was not clean. Bathroom was dirty not what I was expecting at all, but my booking was nonrefundable, so I have no other choice but to stay here that’s the only reason why I had to stay my daughter had a basketball game that weekend and we had to stay we had no other place to go because my state was not refundable. I call Airbnb and they told me that unfortunately was nonrefundable. There was nothing they could do worse experience renting Airbnb that I had. I have never gone through this before there is no pool and the description says there is a pool!!

description: Lovely Ranch home nestled on a quiet street near the SUNY campus. This home has a detached garage, porch and patio.  There's central air, new dishwasher, 55" 4k Smart TV, new furniture, lots of seating and expanding dining table for up to 8+.  Lots of Parking.  2 Queen bedrooms and 2 more beds can be added to one of the common rooms.
review: Fantastic host! Very responsive to questions & requests. House was clean and spacious as described. Location is great...very convenient. Close to everything from restaurants, night life, stores, pharmacy, places of worship, public transportation, University/College...the list goes on. I highly recommend Piotr’s home(s) and will definitely stay again when I am back in the Albany area!

description: Welcome to The Dove’s Nest, a cozy and quiet space perfect for solo travelers or couples. Located in a peaceful neighborhood in Albany, it's a great place to relax or get some work done.  What you’ll get: - Bedroom, Livingroom, and bathroom, - Comfortable bed and linens - Small sitting area - Washer and Dryer - Smart - TV - Wi-Fi - Mini fridge, microwave, and coffee/tea setup - Full Private bathroom  - Shared entrance and available parking  Come enjoy a simple, calm stay at The Dove’s Nest.

review: Musab is a great host. Communication was easy, place was clean and quiet. I enjoyed my stay.

Walking distance to Buckingham Lake. Quiet and peaceful neighborhood. Distance away from a large variety of restaurants and local bars, modern amenities, including two full bathroom with efficient, washer and dryer.
review: if you're looking for a home that is truly a home away from home this is the place. the home is in a great neighborhood very quiet and we Felt extremely safe In our surroundings. Albany New York can be tough in certain neighborhoods but not this one if I could give it 10 Stars I would.

description: Hello, thanks for checking out my listing!  This vintage, updated home has  multiple living areas inc. living room, dining room, enclosed porch & outdoor patio with tree-lined back yard. large bedroom, shared 1.5 baths.   2 mi to Albany Medical, the VA, 1 mi to St Peter's. Approx. 1 mi (walkable!) from University at Albany main campus, Harriman State campus, groceries & nightlife. Please note that the mattress is a short queen.
review: I had an exceptionally comfortable stay at Emily’s place. The location was convenient and I enjoyed walking down the pretty neighborhood to get to the bus stations to go downtown. The house itself and the room I stayed in was very clean and spacious. I must mention that Chuck, her cat, was very sweet and welcoming :D If I’m ever in Albany again, I’m definitely coming back here to stay!

description: Stay above it all in this stylish studio perched above Dove + Deer, Albany’s favorite restaurant. Located in historic Center Square, you’re steps from the Capitol, The Egg, and Washington Park. Enjoy a welcome drink and exclusive dining discounts. The studio offers cozy charm with exposed brick, luxe linens, Wi-Fi, and a kitchenette. Perfect for solo travelers or couples wanting a walkable, vibrant neighborhood with unbeatable food and flair.
review: Thomas and Friends is how I’d describe my stay. When I booked this Airbnb I was determined to finally see Albany to figure out if moving here was in my cards or not. This stay showed me just that. As soon as I arrived I was shown the place by one of the restaurant owners. It’s the cutest space there ever was. I can babble on about my time here, but I simply just want to say that Thomas and Friends are great hosts and even greater people to know. If you stay anywhere stay with them. 💕

description: Entire garden apartment in Albany, New York. Walking distance to restaurants, shops and Washington Park. Situated in the heart of Historic Center Square neighborhood. 15 minute walk or short ride to MVP Arena. Perfect for couples and short getaways, but we welcome longer stays too!
review: Great place! The hosts are amazing! Very kind! Best place in case you are sitting for the Bar exam in Albany and need a cozy and convenient place near the Empire State Plaza or Albany Capital Center. It is a ten-minute walk! No need to get a cab or car! Stress-free! Loved everything about it! Super clean! Safe neighborhood! Felt at home right off! It has everything you need! 1000000 stars!

description: The apartment is located in a quiet neighborhood . Fully furnished. Linens and towels are provided. It's two blocks away from downtown Albany, and the Palace theater.
review: Kenneth was ready when I got there and arranged for the upstairs neighbor to meet me at the door with the keys.  Shortly after that I was left on my own in the privacy of this large 2 BR apartment - just like I like it.   I really like my privacy and there was no time at which anyone came to visit me or bother me unannounced.  At one point Kenneth had to pic up a refrigerator stored in the apartment, but the neighbor lady gave me plenty of notice and I was on my way out before he got there.  When I returned the extra refrigerator was gone and I had the nice quiet apartment to myself.  At one point I went to take a shower and found some body wash in a drawer.  That was nice since all I brought with me was shampoo.    The apartment is a city street apartment meaning that like many two family homes the BR window is feet away from the public sidewalk and you can hear people all hours of the night walking by and conversing, but its not terribly disruptive.  Overall the apartment was just what I needed and I had an enjoyable time while there.  The heat worked perfectly and the apartment was picked up.  The bed was a king size and very comfortable with sensible pillows and coverings.  The water pressure on the shower was awesome.

description: Large private bedroom in a four room apartment, centrally located, with a quick Uber or bus to apartment exam sites, hospitals, and universities.   This unit is newly furnished with new medium firm 12in queen hybrid mattress, new linens, and closet for hanging and luggage storage. Some kitchen basics, gas stove, and stainless fridge available for guest use.
review: Emily is an attentive host; her home was exactly as described. I  was very happy with my air-conditioned room as the days were very hot & humid.    This home is clean, clean, clean & the kitchen is great!

description: 4800ft of living space and an epic backyard make this historic home a true gem. The blueprints for the Jesse Buel House reside in the Library of Congress…for real. The pool is enormous and OPEN TIL early NOV! Hot tub open all year. There is enough furniture for everyone to get cozy. Two kitchens, a billiard room, OLED TVs, luxurious mattresses and eclectic décor make this home truly one of a kind. 35 minutes to Saratoga Race Track with loads of other fantastic attractions within an hour drive.
review: Gorgeous house with everything you could want or need. Thomas was so accommodating and was quick with communication. Hope to book again in the future.

description: Charming 5 room bed and breakfast located across from Washington Park in Albany, NY. Each room has a private bathroom and workstation. Guests have access to a shared kitchen, dining area, living space and small office nook with printer.
review: We are from Binghamton and visiting the area last minute for my daughter's dance competition. I just got out of the hospital and was able to make it up there for the the last day of it.  We had no idea we were driving into single digit temperatures or that everything was covered in ice due to an earlier storm. This storm has knocked the power out of half of the city and unfortunately due to the power outages the code we had been given for the door was reset and didn't work when we arrived much later than we expected due to road conditions. Fortunately another patron of the Inn let us in and our room was open for us to get into.  I messaged Terra to let her know and she called back quickly apologizing for the upset which we had no issue with. She offered recommendations on places that may still be serving food and on her way over even messaged us some.places that she saw we're still open. Her and her husband brought us a key to use and a new front door code. Will def stay again!

description: Brand new finished 900 sq ft Apartment at 199 Lark Street, Albany NY 12210. On-street parking, The most Vibrant Street in Albany.  Walking to Downtown Business District and Washington Park. Enjoy easy access to everything from this perfectly located home base.
review: This spot was perfect for our non-profit team to go to the Capital for work stuff for a few days. It's walking distance to all of the state buidings, and SO many good restaurants. Right next to the apt is an incredible poke bowl spot! highly recommended! Ron went above and beyond as a host, too!

review: This isn't my first rodeo at Dashima's place and I am excited to return when I get a chance again in the near future. She was very responsive to a unexpected event at the home and had a solution within hours. I would recommend!

review: Thank you Rachael you made me feel very welcome in your home! You went above and beyond. Felt like I was visiting distant family. I would highly recommend staying here!

review: Stayed for a month and Michael was a fantastic host! The place is exactly as shown in the pictures. Kitchen was clean, has a great fridge and ovens, and is well stocked with basic tools to cook for yourself if you don't want to burn money ordering out all the time. I was able to get food and deliveries easily to the place, and security was solid. Laundry room was easy to access/use as well. The walls in the bedroom are a little thin. You'll hear just about everything from the units around you, so expect the same vice versa. Otherwise, plenty of space for your stuff and a decent work desk to use as you need. Internet was fantastic for the entire stay. I would definitely recommend the place for a stay!

Very walkable location next to Albany medical center.  ~fast wi-fi ~fully equipped kitchen ~55” smart tv ~free off street parking ~ contactless self check-in
review: I had a great time at Albany working for 8 days based mostly in the apartment.  The location is great! Just a short walk from the center of Albany and there are great restaurants within walking distance.  And the range of cuisines available was a real surprise.  Despite being told to whatever else I did, "Don't turn the bottom lock!" Yes, I turned the bottom lock and locked myself out. The hostess was there within 30 minutes to let me back in with a smile! Great service. The apartment was adequately furnished with a good TV, comfortable sofa, really comfortable bed and an entertaining shower. Would I stay again?  Absolutely. Thanks for a really nice stay.

Traveling nurses & Dr. are welcome for long term stays. It features a bedroom with a queen-size bed, a living room, a kitchen, a bathroom, TVs, Wi-Fi, Mini-Billiard table,  a washer/dryer, A/C & heat, a Gas fireplace, off-street parking space. The patio with a table and parasol offers a breathtaking view of landscape.
review: Joe was very kind and responsible during our stay. The surrounding environment was peaceful, nature-friendly, and the air was fresh. The accommodation was also great, and the sofa was extremely comfortable. The kitchen was well-equipped with various cooking utensils, which made cooking very convenient. If I have the chance to return, I would love to stay here again.!

description: Discover a bright, thoughtfully designed basement hideaway just minutes from Albany’s Warehouse District. Enjoy a reclining sofa, 50" Roku TV, full kitchen with ice-making fridge and dishwasher, and a stylish bathroom with gold finishes and a ring-lit mirror. Remote-controlled A/C and heat, private digital entry, and fast Wi-Fi make it easy to relax. Walk or drive to breweries, downtown venues, and more.
review: This is by far the best airbnb where I have stayed. The studio looks in pristine condition exactly like on the pictures✨✨. The design is really modern with a luxurious aesthetic. It's very quiet very private with its own entry way. The place comes fully equipped, the bed is really comfy like hotel bed there is a big tv in the living room, the couch is really comfortable and there is also a washer dryer in the unit which is a big plus. The host is very responsive and very friendly. He was able to quickly answer all the questions I had. Overall, I would rate my stay 10 out of 10👌🏼. I thoroughly enjoyed it and I recommend it to anyone looking for a peaceful and relaxing retreat.

description: The whole group will enjoy easy access to everything from this centrally located place. Large kitchen open to living room makes a great space for hanging out while making meals & memories together.  Located on 1st floor so no steps to navigate making it easy to access. First bedroom offers a spacious king size bed. Second bedroom has a queen with everything you need for a relaxing get away. Great value for area. Stay a night, two or more. Here to meet your needs.
review: Had a great couple nights. Diana was very helpful with any question I had. Would definitely stay here again!

description: Place is within 2-3 miles of AMC, St. Peter's,  UAlbany, SUNY, College of St. Rose, Washington park, Empire State Plaza, and many other establishments.  Only 15 minute ride to the airport !
review: My stay with FS in Albany was one of the best Airbnbs I have ever booked. Coming to Albany, I was doubly nervous—I had an exam to take and was visiting the city for the first time—but I immediately felt at home. FS was a fantastic host—super helpful, offering assistance with transport and navigating the city. Most of all, it was a pleasure to share a cup of tea with him, especially during the stressful exam period. The room was excellent, and both the kitchen and restroom were in pristine condition. The location was perfect, providing easy access to my exam venue. If a six-star rating were possible, I would give it without hesitation!

description: This elegant apartment on the ground floor of an historic 3-story brick row house in the heart of downtown Albany NY, opens to a beautifully appointed living/dining area, has a full kitchen, 2 spacious bedrooms, and a full bath. The fully-equipped kitchen opens to a comfortable living/dining area. This cozy, modern space offers fast wifi, streaming services, plush bedding, full bath, in-unit washer and dryer, and is just steps from Café Capriccio. Please click on photos for more information.
review: Absolutely love four stay here. The host were amazing and so was the restaurant! We loved our stay here!

review: Antonio has a wonderful space. He has great hospitality. It’s in college town but the noise was minimal.

description: Spacious Pine Hills flat with many recent updates including refinished hardwood floors, all new windows and a completely remodeled kitchen, including brand new appliances. This unit also has a private laundry room as well as an entrance foyer. There is off street parking and the building is located within walking distance to 2 primary bus routes. This unit is also within walking distance of popular restaurants, Juniors, The Point, Cafe Madison and Kismet Mediterranean Grill.
review: This apartment was amazing! Very large, stylish, and updated. Easily could accommodate a family comfortably. Every amenity you could possibly need is included. The owners responded to all requests immediately and professionally. I stayed for an extended stay and was extremely happy. Highly recommended!

description: Step into Fortune Suites, a recently revamped Two-bedroom apartment ideally situated within 8 to 10 minutes' drive of Albany Med, Times Union Center, and other local attractions. Rest assured, high-speed internet is included for your convenience. Welcome and enjoy your stay!
review: It was exactly what we needed for short time we stayed. Wasnt more wasnt less.The pictures look exactly how it is. Osas’s is definitely a great host! From attempting to check in to requesting a late check out he was there assisting over the phone and thats always welcoming. The neighborhood does look a little sketchy, in my opinion it just relects it being a low income neighborhood.

description: Welcome to your cozy Albany retreat! Enjoy a comfortable home in this well-appointed space, perfect for travelers looking for convenience and relaxation. Your booking includes free parking and a complimentary breakfast to start your day right. Located near shops, restaurants, and downtown Albany, this home offers easy access to local attractions. Whether you’re here for business or leisure, you’ll love the comfort and convenience of this stay. Book now for a relaxing experience!
review: Zohar was an awesome host! The place was pretty decent, though. It’s in an older building, but it was surprisingly clean. The sink drain was a bit slow, but I was there for two days and didn’t have to use the sink much. So, it was totally understandable. I highly recommend it!

review: It is a Ramada Inn room and it is very old. The carpet is dirty and the ceiling popcorn is peeling off in areas and or completely broken off exposing the sheetrock on the ceiling. The bathroom tub was clogged when I used the shower. The bed was comfortable enough but very old and it has old blackened chewed out wholes on the side of the mattress where the seams are. Thankfully I stayed for only one night because I checked in late and didn't have another option. If I had an option I would have left. This is not someone's "home" this is an old stained room in an old unkept Ramada Inn.

description: Charming 5 room bed and breakfast located across from Washington Park in Albany, NY. Each room has a private bathroom and workstation. Guests have access to a shared kitchen, dining area, living space and small office nook with printer.
review: Basic BnB in a lovely building. Worked great for a short stay. Thank you!

review: Beautiful location in historic building at the entrance to a large city park. It took about ten minutes to walk through Washington Park to get to shops and eateries on Lark Street. Ground floor room was on the small side but included a large bathroom with walk-in shower. Fine for me (solo traveler). Free parking on site was a plus. If you like architecture, tons of interesting buildings and houses all within walking distance.

Whether you’re here for business or leisure, you’ll love the comfort and convenience of this stay. Book now for a relaxing experience.
review: The place was double booked. When we were assigned to a new room, we opened the door and there was another guest already in that room. Then, we got assigned to another new room, which had a difficult lock, different size bed, and no kitchen as advertised. We did end up staying and the room was okay, besides a bit of dust/hair. For a last minute booking for one night, it sufficed. It was the cheapest price in the city, but still a bit expensive for what we got considering the issues.

description: Forget your worries in this spacious and serene space. Located close to all major highways, hospitals, shopping and historic downtown Albany. Boast 2 decks and large front porch for relaxation or entertaining. Has large park and large yard space on quiet dead-end street. Family friendly with great neighbors. Close to bus line. Very spacious with all new appliances and all amenities new as well. You will have a great stay in this upgraded beautiful home.
review: Patrice is a wonderful host. She is very responsive and goes above and beyond to make the stay stress-free. Her home is well equipped for a family, and we were very happy with our stay.

description: Centrally located building, this room has lots of light and ample area to move. Common areas shared with other guest. Close to mayor highways, restaurants, colleges and hospitals,  beautifully Central Park, Albany Museum, Empire State Plaza and the historic District is minutes away. Welcome.  Public transportation is available.
review: At first glance, it looks like an ok place to live, but don't be fooled by the pictures! She put some cheap decorations, but it's dusty and dark everywhere. The house is really old, and many things are broken. The dining room window is cracked and held together with super glue. The washer is moldy; the sheets made me itchy. I washed them, but I got even itchier afterwards. The house next door looks abandoned. The neighborhood is "safe" for Albany, but you can't take the bus that in the morning - it's a school shuttle for kids only so it's inconvenient.  The listing doesn't mention the steep stairs you have to go up to get to the bedrooms. The railing is broken so it's a safety hazard - you can fall off and injure yourself if you're not careful. You need to provide your own toilet paper. The bathroom has cockroaches and spiders. The bedroom is dark and has only one small window. The dedicated workspace is a closet with a desk and no electric lighting, making it impossible to use.

description: Beautiful and spacious 2 bedroom, 1.5 bathroom in a downtown historic brownstone. Recently renovated with modern updates. 2 floors of living space. Outdoor space. Walking distance to many points of interests, restaurants, bars, and breweries.
review: My 2 daughters and I stayed here to attend a dance competition and the #1 excellent thing about this home is that it was very safe, solidly built with pinpad entry, good locks and security camera. The space itself was  exceptuonally clean, bright and spacious with very comfortable furniture and beautiful, contemporary design elements. The full kitchen allowed us to save money by cooking and all the appliances were clean and modern and easy to use. The open air balcony was perfect for relaxing in the fresh air- at night it was well lit and we sat and played cards out there &  enjoyed the view of the architecture and city lights. The street is quiet & mattresses were so comfortable & we slept peacefully. The large windows and high ceilings were like going back in time to a historic property but with all the comforts of modern living. There was parking right in front of the home and the theater &  sevetal good restaurants are wihin walking distance. We look forward to staying here again!

description: Stay in a clean, quiet home with 2 friendly housemates. Renovated in 2023, this co-living space offers: 	•	Private bedroom (furnished) 	•	Shared bathroom 	•	Fully furnished common areas 	•	Fast Wi-Fi 	•	In-home laundry 	•	off-street parking 	•	Shared entry  Perfect for short or long stays. Convenient, comfortable, and move-in ready!   You will be centrally located 10–15 mins to 3 hospitals, medical centers, downtown Albany, night-life and both shopping malls.
review: It is a very clean and new space. Felt super comfortable. In-unit laundry is a huge plus. You would find everything you need for cooking, washing dishes, washing laundry, etc. Extra towels and cleaning supplies available. The place look exactly like the photos and nothing less. Alain is a very responsive host. I’m really happy I chose this place and would highly recommend it to a clean-loving person.

review: I hate to write bad review because I understand hosting on Airbnb is really not easy. Unfortunately, my experience with the last night was totally unsatisfactory. Upon arrival, the property had a persistent foul and musty odor, making the space unpleasant to stay in. The furniture and amenities were outdated, with clear signs of wear and lack of maintenance. One of the  bed frames creaked with every movement, the mattress was too worn out and sunken in the middle, affecting the quality of rest. The noisy air conditioners were behind the curtains which made the air vent working poorly.  We checked in at 2:30 am, otherwise we would have not spent 10 minutes inside the apartment. The host charged us more than a nightly rate that made the experience even more unpleasant.  Overall, this property did not reflect the standards or conditions advertised in the listing. For future guests, I would strongly recommend improvements in cleanliness, maintenance, and accuracy of the description.

description: The whole group will enjoy easy access to everything from this centrally located place. Large kitchen open to living room makes a great space for hanging out while making meals & memories together.  Located on 1st floor so no steps to navigate making it easy to access. First bedroom offers a spacious king size bed. Second bedroom has a queen with everything you need for a relaxing get away. Great value for area. Stay a night, two or more. Here to meet your needs.
review: Big space and comfy beds. Diana was great. Not a great neighborhood. The upstairs neighbors were very noisy.
